description: Google Maps accommodation discovery tool using Google Places API (New) for data fetching, with interactive Explorer UI — see SPEC.md for full specification, DEVELOPMENT.md for implementation guide

userStories.title: Project restructure — monorepo setup
userStories.description: Set up monorepo with server/ and client/ packages. Move existing MVP to legacy/. Server: Express + TypeScript. Client: Vite + React + TypeScript. Root npm workspaces. .env.example with GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install from root succeeds
server/ and client/ each have package.json and tsconfig.json
legacy/src/index.ts contains the original MVP code
npm run typecheck passes in both server/ and client/
npm run build succeeds in both packages
server starts and responds to GET /health with 200
client dev server starts and renders a placeholder page
.env.example exists with GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY placeholder

userStories.title: SQLite database schema and data access layer

Schema creates all tables: projects, scrape_runs, tiles, places, reviews, place_scrape_runs, shortlists, shortlist_entries
Typed CRUD functions exist for each entity
Tests pass: create DB, insert/read/update/delete for Project and Place entities
npm run typecheck passes in server/
Database file is created in a configurable location (default: data/gomaps.db)

userStories.title: Express API skeleton with project CRUD

Placeholder routers mounted at /api/scrape, /api/places, /api/shortlists

userStories.title: Migrate server TypeScript to Effect-TS
userStories.description: Migrate the server/ package from plain TypeScript to Effect. Replace try/catch with typed Effect errors, wrap DB access in Effect services with Layer-based dependency injection, use Effect.gen for async flows, and adopt Effect Schema for API request/response validation.
acceptanceCriteria: effect installed in server/
DB access layer (server/src/db/) wrapped as Effect services with typed errors (DbError, NotFoundError)
Express route handlers use Effect.runPromise at the API boundary
API request validation uses Effect Schema instead of manual checks
All existing API endpoints preserved (no regressions)
npm run typecheck --workspace=server passes
npm test --workspace=server passes
userStories.notes: Effect-TS v3.19.19 installed. All DB functions return Effect<A, DbError | NotFoundError, Db>. Typed errors in server/src/errors.ts.

userStories.title: Places API client module and Playwright removal
userStories.description: Replace the Playwright-based scraper with a Google Places API (New) HTTP client. Create server/src/scraper/places-api.ts wrapping Text Search (POST /v1/places:searchText) and Place Details (GET /v1/places/{placeId}). Use field masks to control SKU costs. Parse API responses into DB types. Remove Playwright dependency. Rename Place.googleUrl to Place.googleMapsUri throughout codebase (types, DB mapper, schema column, routes, tests). Place IDs now use Google Place ID (e.g., 'ChIJ...'). Remove old engine.ts and utils.ts. Use Effect patterns consistent with existing codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/scraper/places-api.ts exports textSearch() and getPlaceDetails() functions returning Effect types
textSearch() calls POST https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:searchText with API key, query, locationBias, and field mask
getPlaceDetails() calls GET https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places/{placeId} with API key and field mask
API responses parsed into CreatePlaceInput shape for DB insertion

Field masks configured to request: displayName, location, rating, userRatingCount, formattedAddress, internationalPhoneNumber, websiteUri, primaryTypeDisplayName, priceLevel, googleMapsUri, reviews, photos, regularOpeningHours
Old server/src/scraper/engine.ts (Playwright) removed
Old server/src/scraper/utils.ts removed
playwright removed from server/package.json dependencies
Place.googleUrl renamed to Place.googleMapsUri in types.ts, places.ts DB functions, schema.ts (column rename: google_url → google_maps_uri), route files, and tests
Place ID uses Google Place ID format instead of SHA-256 hash
Unit tests pass with mocked HTTP responses for both textSearch and getPlaceDetails
npm run typecheck --workspace=server passes
npm test --workspace=server passes
userStories.notes: Use native fetch() for HTTP calls. API key from process.env.GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY. Wrap in Effect.tryPromise for error handling. See SPEC.md §7 for field mapping table.

userStories.title: Adaptive tiling algorithm
userStories.description: Implement server/src/scraper/tiling.ts. Given bounding box, generate coarse grid (~0.1°). Process tiles sequentially. If Text Search result count >= 60 (API pagination limit), subdivide into 4 sub-tiles. Track tile status in DB. Enforce minimum tile size floor. See SPEC.md §5.
acceptanceCriteria: generateTiles(bounds) returns initial grid of tile objects
subdivideTile(tile) returns 4 child tiles
shouldSubdivide(resultCount, threshold) returns boolean with default threshold of 60
Minimum tile size floor (0.01°) prevents infinite recursion
Tile status tracked in SQLite: pending, running, completed, subdivided
Progress can be queried: tiles total/completed/subdivided, places found
Unit tests pass for tile generation, subdivision, bounds math
npm run typecheck --workspace=server passes
npm test --workspace=server passes
userStories.notes: Threshold is 60 (API pagination limit of 3 pages × 20 results), not 120 (old browser scraping limit).

userStories.title: Scrape engine orchestrator using Places API
userStories.description: Implement server/src/scraper/engine.ts as the orchestrator that combines tiling + Places API client + DB layer. For each tile: call Text Search with locationBias, paginate results (up to 3 pages), check subdivision threshold, insert/update places in DB. Then optionally fetch Place Details for enriched data (reviews, photos). De-duplicate by Google Place ID. Checkpoint/resume via tile status. Rate limiting between requests.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/scraper/engine.ts exports a startScrape(config) function returning Effect
Config accepts: scrapeRunId, query, bounds, optional delayMs (default 200ms)
Engine generates tiles for bounds, iterates tiles sequentially
For each tile: calls textSearch with locationBias, paginates via nextPageToken
Places inserted into DB via createPlace(), reviews via createReview()
De-duplication: existing places (by Google Place ID) are linked but not re-scraped
Subdivision: tiles with 60+ results are subdivided into 4 sub-tiles
Tile status updated in DB after each tile (checkpoint/resume)
ScrapeRun progress updated: placesFound, placesUnique, tilesCompleted
Configurable delay between API requests for rate limiting
Website classification applied to each place via classifier (US-008 dependency — skip if not yet implemented)
npm run typecheck --workspace=server passes
npm test --workspace=server passes
userStories.notes: This replaces the old Playwright-based engine. Uses Effect patterns. Depends on US-005 (places-api.ts) and US-006 (tiling.ts).

userStories.title: Website classification
userStories.description: Implement server/src/scraper/classifier.ts. Classify place website domain as direct/ota/social/unknown. Hardcoded allowlist of ~30 OTA and social domains. See SPEC.md §7 classification table.
acceptanceCriteria: classifyWebsite(url) returns 'direct' | 'ota' | 'social' | 'unknown'
Correctly classifies: booking.com, airbnb.com, expedia.com, hotels.com, vrbo.com, agoda.com, tripadvisor.com → ota
Correctly classifies: facebook.com, instagram.com → social
Correctly classifies: www.hotel-example.it → direct
Handles subdomains: www.booking.com → ota
Handles null/undefined input → unknown
Place records include websiteType field populated by classifier
Comprehensive tests pass (15+ test cases)
npm run typecheck --workspace=server passes
npm test --workspace=server passes

userStories.title: Scrape API — start, monitor, pause with SSE progress
userStories.description: Wire scrape engine into Express API. POST to start scrape (projectId + query). GET scrape run status. POST to pause/resume. SSE endpoint for live progress. Engine runs as background async task in server process.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/scrape/start accepts { projectId, query } and returns scrapeRunId
GET /api/scrape/:runId returns run status, tile progress, place counts
POST /api/scrape/:runId/pause pauses the running scrape
POST /api/scrape/:runId/resume resumes a paused scrape
GET /api/scrape/:runId/progress is an SSE endpoint streaming progress events
Progress events include: { tilesCompleted, tilesTotal, tilesSubdivided, placesFound, placesUnique, elapsedMs }
Scrape runs in background — API remains responsive during scrape

npm test --workspace=server passes

userStories.title: Setup hardened UI testing mechanics with Playwright
userStories.description: Install and configure Playwright for end-to-end browser testing of the React client. Create test infrastructure: base fixtures with browser context isolation, automatic console-error capture (fail on React ErrorBoundary or severe errors), screenshot helpers (before/after each major interaction), network-idle waiters, and a shared page-object layer using the strict locator hierarchy (data-testid > role > text). Add npm scripts for running e2e tests. Configure Playwright to proxy API requests to the dev server. Ensure all test utilities follow the conventions in AGENTS.md § LLM Agent Testing Guide and § Tooling & Execution Mechanics.
acceptanceCriteria: @playwright/test installed in client/ (or root)
playwright.config.ts configured: baseURL pointing to Vite dev server, webServer auto-start for both client and server
Base test fixture: fresh browser context per test, clears cookies/localStorage for isolation
Console-error listener fixture: captures page errors, fails test on React ErrorBoundary or severe console errors
Screenshot utility: helper to capture full-page screenshots at named steps (e.g., step-1-before.png)
Locator helpers following strict hierarchy: prefer getByTestId, then getByRole, then getByText
waitForNetworkIdle and waitForVisible helper utilities (no hardcoded sleeps)
npm run test:e2e script runs Playwright tests
At least one smoke test: navigates to /, verifies Projects page renders
npm run typecheck passes (if TS config covers test files)
userStories.notes: Top priority. Foundation for all subsequent UI tests. Follow AGENTS.md § LLM Agent Testing Guide and § Tooling & Execution Mechanics (Playwright) conventions strictly.

userStories.title: Write rigorous end-to-end tests for all existing features
userStories.description: Using the Playwright infrastructure from US-026, write comprehensive e2e tests covering every implemented feature and every user interaction that is available in the browser UI. Follow the AGENTS.md § LLM Agent Testing Guide checklist systematically. Tests must cover: project CRUD (create, open, delete), map rendering and area selection on Setup page, scrape launch with live SSE progress, Explorer page (map markers, clustering, table rendering, sorting), map↔table selection sync, detail panel content, and navigation between pages. Each test must use proper waiting strategies, take verification screenshots, and monitor for console errors. Add data-testid attributes to components where needed for reliable selection.
acceptanceCriteria: Project management tests: create project, verify in list, open project navigates to Setup, delete project with confirmation

Every interactive control visible in browser UI is exercised by at least one e2e assertion path (buttons, links, inputs, selects, toggles, sortable headers, table rows, marker clicks, keyboard-accessible card actions)
No interaction may be marked as covered implicitly; each interaction has an explicit action + expected result assertion
Setup page tests: Google Maps renders and is interactive, area selection creates draggable rectangle, coordinates pill updates, clear removes selection
Scrape tests: enter query, click Start Scrape, verify progress panel appears with SSE updates, tile overlay colors change, pause/resume controls work
Explorer map tests: markers render with correct color coding (green/yellow/red by rating), clustering shows numeric counts when zoomed out
Explorer table tests: columns render correctly, sorting toggles ascending/descending, virtual scrolling handles large datasets, filter input narrows results
Selection sync tests: clicking table row centers map and opens detail panel, clicking map marker highlights table row and opens detail panel
Detail panel tests: displays name, rating, address, amenities, photos, reviews; website badge color-coded correctly; action buttons construct correct URLs
Navigation tests: URL routes update during navigation, breadcrumbs are correct
All tests use strict locator hierarchy (data-testid > role > text)
No hardcoded sleeps — all waits use DOM assertions or network-idle
Screenshots captured at key verification points
Console errors monitored and cause test failure
A coverage checklist artifact is maintained in the test suite mapping each interactive UI element to the e2e test(s) that validate it
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Coverage expanded in client/e2e/tests/app-flows.spec.ts (project CRUD, setup progress, explorer sorting/filter/selection) and checklist maintained in client/e2e/tests/coverage-checklist.md. Playwright now loads root .env in config so map-capable runs use VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY consistently.

userStories.title: Fix project/run status consistency and harden cross-view progress truth
userStories.description: Resolve data consistency bugs between Projects and Setup views so project status badges, project summary metadata, and active run progress always reflect the same authoritative backend state. Specifically address the observed mismatch where a project card shows Complete while Setup still has active/incomplete runs, project summary incorrectly shows Places: 0 / Scrape runs: 0 / Last scraped: never despite existing runs, and Setup progress remains stuck at 77% from a previous run while a different selected run is active. Define deterministic status derivation rules and ensure run-scoped progress is never polluted by another run's counters.
acceptanceCriteria: Projects page status badge derives from authoritative run state (Running if any run for project is running; Complete only when latest relevant run is completed and no run is active; Failed/Paused reflected correctly)
Project card summary metrics (Places, Scrape runs, Last scraped) are sourced from persisted project/run/place data and match backend values for non-empty projects
Setup page progress panel is strictly bound to the selected/active run ID and never displays counters or percentage from another run
Switching between historical runs in Previous Runs updates progress/tile counters deterministically and immediately
When a new run starts, progress initializes from that run's counters and cannot inherit stale percentage/time from prior runs
Server API contract is explicit about project aggregate fields and run status precedence to prevent client-side guesswork
Add regression tests at API level for project aggregate summary and status derivation (including mixed completed/failed/running run history)
Add Playwright end-to-end regressions that recreate the Sardinia mismatch scenario and assert consistency between Projects card, Setup Previous Runs, and Setup Progress panel
Extend the UI interaction coverage checklist to include cross-view consistency assertions for status badges, summary metrics, and run-scoped progress
Run and pass: npm run typecheck --workspace=server, npm run typecheck --workspace=client, npm test --workspace=server, npm run test:e2e
userStories.notes: Completed with backend-derived /api/projects aggregates (status + places/runs/lastScraped + activeRunId), Setup run-scoped progress reset on run switch, API regression coverage in server/tests/projects.test.ts, and Playwright Sardinia consistency regression in client/e2e/tests/app-flows.spec.ts.

userStories.title: E2E story-boards: Projects Page edge cases
userStories.description: Add Playwright e2e tests in client/e2e/tests/ covering untested Projects Page scenarios. Use seeded fixtures, strict locator hierarchy, no hardcoded sleeps, screenshots at key points, console-error monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Project card aggregate fields: seed projects with each status variant (draft/running/paused/failed/complete), verify status badge text and CSS class, placesCount, scrapeRunsCount, lastScrapedAt render correctly for each
Project card map preview: verify mini Google Maps preview renders when API key is present, or fallback text when absent
Project card hover selection: hover a card, verify is-selected class applied and 'Selected: {name}' copy updates
Multiple projects ordering: create 3 projects, verify list order matches creation order
Rapid create-delete cycle: create a project and immediately delete it, verify no race-condition errors or leftover cards
Create with duplicate name: create two projects with the same name, verify both appear in the list (or verify error if the server rejects duplicates)
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Completed with `client/e2e/tests/projects-edge-cases.spec.ts` covering status aggregates, map preview/fallback, hover selection glow, newest-first ordering, rapid create/delete, and duplicate-name creation, plus targeted Projects test ids and deterministic `created_at` ordering tie-breaker in `/api/projects`.

userStories.title: E2E story-boards: Setup Page validation, estimates, and chrome
userStories.description: Add Playwright e2e tests covering Setup Page UI elements that are not yet exercised: estimate badge, empty-query guard, breadcrumbs, runs list cap, and bounds persistence round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: Estimate badge: select area, verify setup-estimate-badge shows '~N tiles · Est. M min'; clear area, verify fallback text 'Select an area to estimate tiles and timing'
Start scrape with empty query: clear the query input, click Start Scrape, verify error message appears ('Enter a query…') and no /api/scrape/start request fires
Start scrape disabled without area: with no bounds selected, verify setup-start-scrape-button is disabled
Breadcrumbs: verify breadcrumb text renders 'Projects / {project name} / Setup' with the correct seeded project name
Runs list capped at 6: seed 8 runs for one project, verify only 6 are rendered in setup-runs-section
Bounds persistence round-trip: select area on map → navigate to Projects → navigate back to Setup → verify coordinates pill and rectangle are restored from persisted bounds
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Completed with `client/e2e/tests/setup-validation.spec.ts` covering estimate badge + fallback, empty-query guard without firing `/api/scrape/start`, disabled start without bounds, breadcrumb chrome, runs-list cap at 6, and map-driven bounds persistence round-trip. Added `setup-breadcrumbs` test id and checklist mappings.

userStories.title: E2E story-boards: Setup Page run status, progress, and SSE
userStories.description: Add Playwright e2e tests covering Setup Page run status badges, progress bar rendering, elapsed/ETA display, pause/resume label toggling, tile overlay colors, SSE progress subscription, and map diagnostic messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Run status badges: seed runs with each status (pending/running/paused/completed/failed), verify each run button shows correct status text and CSS class (setup-run-status-{status})
Progress bar: seed a running run at ~50%, verify setup-progress-fill has is-running class and width style approximately 50%
Elapsed + ETA: seed a running run with non-zero elapsedMs and partial completion, verify 'Time: Xm Ys · Est. remaining …' renders in setup-progress-stats
Pause → Resume label toggle: click pause button, verify label changes to 'Pausing…' then 'Resume'; click again, verify 'Resuming…' then 'Pause'
Tile overlay colors (interactive map only): seed tiles with completed/running/pending/subdivided statuses, verify green/yellow/gray rectangles render on map
SSE progress subscription: mock /api/scrape/:runId/progress SSE endpoint, push a progress event, verify setup-progress-section updates without page reload
Map diagnostic messages: test the 3 diagnostic branches (API key error via onError, map init timeout, tile load timeout) by mocking/controlling map load timing
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Completed with `client/e2e/tests/setup-progress.spec.ts` covering run status badge classes, 50% running progress + ETA, pause/resume label transitions via stateful route mocks, SSE-driven progress updates, interactive tile-overlay color/state assertions, and the three map diagnostic branches. Added Playwright-only Setup hooks (`VITE_E2E_TEST_MODE`, `e2eMapDiagnostic` query param, `setup-tile-overlay-debug`) plus checklist mappings.

userStories.title: E2E story-boards: Explorer detail panel, badges, and formatting
userStories.description: Add Playwright e2e tests covering Explorer Page detail panel content, website type badge CSS classes, price level formatting, marker color by rating, and empty/edge states.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail panel full content: seed a place with all fields (name, category, rating, address, phone, website, websiteType, amenities, photoUrls, openingHours), verify every field renders in explorer-detail-panel
Website type badge colors: seed 3 places with websiteType direct/ota/social, verify CSS classes explorer-website-direct, explorer-website-ota, explorer-website-social are applied in the table
Price level formatting: seed places with priceLevel values '$', '$$', '$$$', 'PRICE_LEVEL_INEXPENSIVE', null — verify formatted display in table cells (dollar signs or 'Inexpensive' or '—')
Marker color by rating (interactive map only): seed places with ratings <3.5, 3.5–4.2, >4.2, verify marker icon fillColor matches red/yellow/green
Empty project explorer: navigate to explorer for a project with 0 places, verify '0 places' count and empty table body
Explorer without projects: navigate to /explorer with no projects seeded, verify 'No projects' in dropdown and graceful empty state (no crash)
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Completed with `client/e2e/tests/explorer-detail.spec.ts` covering full detail-panel content assertions, website badge class variants, price-level formatting cases, interactive marker color mapping via `explorer-marker-debug`, empty-project explorer state, and bare `/explorer` empty-project handling. Added Explorer detail test ids and checklist mappings.

userStories.title: E2E story-boards: Explorer map interaction and table-map sync
userStories.description: Add Playwright e2e tests covering Explorer map-marker click → table/detail sync, map-click deselect, selection circle, marker clustering, table-filter vs map-marker isolation, and combined search + table filter composition.
acceptanceCriteria: Map marker click → table + detail sync (interactive map): click a map marker, verify the corresponding table row gets data-selected='true' and detail panel shows that place's name
Map click deselects: with a place selected, click empty map area, verify selection clears (no row selected, detail panel shows placeholder)
Selection circle pulse: select a place, verify a google.maps.Circle is added at the place's coordinates (check via map instance query or visual assertion)
Marker clustering: seed 200+ places, set map zoom to low level, verify clusterer renders cluster markers with numeric count labels
Table filter does not affect map markers: apply table-filter-input, verify map still shows all globally-searched places (table filter is table-only, global search affects both)
Combined search + table filter: apply global search to narrow to ~10 places, then apply table filter within those, verify both compose correctly and explorer-table-count reflects the intersection
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Part 5 of 7 E2E story-board expansion. Add to a new spec file (e.g., explorer-map-sync.spec.ts). Map-interaction tests only run when VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is set; skip gracefully otherwise.

userStories.title: E2E story-boards: Navigation, routing, and accessibility
userStories.description: Add Playwright e2e tests covering navigation edge cases, deep links, redirects, browser back/forward, keyboard-only navigation, and accessibility landmarks.
acceptanceCriteria: Bare /setup redirects to /projects
Bare /explorer without projectId: navigates to explorer page, auto-selects first project if any exist
Deep link to setup with invalid projectId: navigate to /projects/nonexistent/setup, verify 'Project not found.' message
Deep link to explorer with projectId: navigate directly to /projects/{id}/explorer, verify project is selected in dropdown and places load
Nav active link highlight: navigate between pages, verify active NavLink has font-weight 700 and color #1a73e8
Browser back/forward: navigate Projects → Setup → Explorer, press back twice, verify correct pages render at each step
Avatar badge: verify nav-avatar renders with 'U' label
Explorer table keyboard row navigation: Tab to table rows, verify focus is visible; Enter to select a row, verify detail panel opens
Screen reader landmarks: verify role='main' on page containers, role='button' on project cards, aria-label on key containers (projects list, map panels)
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Part 6 of 7 E2E story-board expansion. Add to a new spec file (e.g., navigation-a11y.spec.ts).

userStories.title: E2E story-boards: Error handling, data isolation, and resilience
userStories.description: Add Playwright e2e tests covering API failure scenarios, cross-project data isolation, concurrent runs, and cascading delete verification.
acceptanceCriteria: API down during project load: mock /api/projects as 500, verify projects-error message renders, UI does not crash
API down during explorer data load: mock /api/places as 500, verify explorer-error message renders
API down during scrape start: mock /api/scrape/start as 500, verify setup-error message appears and start button re-enables
API timeout during bounds save: mock slow PUT /api/projects/:id response, verify 'Saving bounds…' copy appears
Multi-project place isolation: seed 2 projects with different places, switch between them in explorer, verify table only shows the active project's places (count assertion)
Concurrent scrape runs on same project: seed 2 running runs for one project, verify setup UI shows both in runs list and switching between them updates progress correctly
Cascading delete: seed a project with runs/tiles/places, delete the project via API, verify GET /api/projects returns empty and GET /api/places?projectId returns empty
Favorite toggle off: star a place then un-star it, verify aria-pressed returns to 'false' and icon changes back to ☆
Sort stability: sort by rating on a dataset with ties, verify tied items are sorted alphabetically by name as tiebreaker
Sort direction toggle: click same column header twice, verify indicator changes ↑ → ↓ and row order reverses
All tests pass: npm run test:e2e succeeds
userStories.notes: Part 7 of 7 E2E story-board expansion. Add to a new spec file (e.g., error-resilience.spec.ts). Use page.route() mocking for API failure scenarios.
